configurePresets.name: macos
configurePresets.description: Activate the einsums-dev conda env FIRST (conda activate einsums-dev), then: cmake --preset macos. Configuring without the env caches Apple's SDK Accelerate BLAS (silently wrong float BLAS-1 results) and fails on OpenMP; if that happened, purge with cmake -U '*BLAS*' -U '*LAPACK*' and reconfigure inside the env.

configurePresets.name: linux
configurePresets.description: Activate the einsums-dev conda env FIRST (conda activate einsums-dev), then: cmake --preset linux. The env supplies the pinned compilers, BLAS/LAPACK, HDF5, and OpenMP; configuring outside it finds system libraries that CI does not test against.

configurePresets.name: windows-clang-cl
configurePresets.description: Activate the conda env first, then configure via devtools/configure_windows.bat (or: cmake -E env --unset=CMAKE_GENERATOR_PLATFORM --unset=CMAKE_GENERATOR_TOOLSET --unset=CMAKE_GENERATOR cmake --preset windows-clang-cl). The unset dance is required because conda-forge's vs2022 activation exports VS-generator settings that Ninja rejects, and CMake treats an empty CMAKE_GENERATOR_PLATFORM as still specified - a preset cannot neutralize it.
